diff --git a/fglab/Dockerfile b/fglab/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..68f91c5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/fglab/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Start with Node.js base image
+FROM node
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Clone FGLab repo and move into it
+RUN cd /root && git clone https://github.com/Kaixhin/FGLab.git && cd FGLab && \
+# npm install
+  npm install && \
+# Create .env with port
+  echo "PORT=5000" > .env
+
+# Expose port
+EXPOSE 5000
+# Set working directory
+WORKDIR /root/FGLab
+# Start server
+ENTRYPOINT ["node", "index.js"]

    Add CUDA versions to neon

diff --git a/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/Dockerfile b/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/Dockerfile
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..fd625a2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda:6.5
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install build-essential, git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  build-essential \
+  git \
+  python-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  python-virtualenv \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libyaml-dev
+
+# Clone neon repo and move into it
+RUN cd /root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" sysinstall
+# Set ~/neon as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/neon
diff --git a/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/README.md b/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/README.md
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..3977e4b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/cuda_v6.5/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+cuda-neon
+=========
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 6.5.14](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [neon](http://neon.nervanasys.com/).
+
+Requirements
+------------
+
+- Host with corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 340.29) installed for the kernel module.
+- Maxwell GPU architecture.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-neon`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).
diff --git a/cuda-neon/cuda_v7.0/Dockerfile b/cuda-neon/cuda_v7.0/Dockerfile
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..9ad29da
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/cuda_v7.0/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda:7.0
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install build-essential, git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  build-essential \
+  git \
+  python-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  python-virtualenv \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libyaml-dev
+
+# Clone neon repo and move into it
+RUN cd /root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" sysinstall
+# Set ~/neon as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/neon

 dockerfiles
 ===========
 
-Compilation of Dockerfiles with automated builds enabled on the [Docker Hub](https://registry.hub.docker.com/repos/kaixhin/).
+Compilation of Dockerfiles with automated builds enabled on the [Docker Hub](https://registry.hub.docker.com/repos/kaixhin/). **Not suitable for production environments.** These images are under continuous development, so breaking changes may be introduced.
 
 All images are based on Ubuntu Core 14.04 LTS, built with minimising size/layers and [best practices](https://docs.docker.com/articles/dockerfile_best-practices/) in mind. Dependencies are indicated left to right e.g. cuda-vnc is VNC built on top of CUDA. Explicit dependencies are excluded. CUDA images can be used without NVIDIA devices attached, but will obviously lack the ability to use CUDA capabilities.
 
-Please note that these images are under continuous development, so breaking changes may be introduced. Therefore, it is not recommended to use these in a production environment without testing.
-
 Up-to-date builds
 -----------------
 
@@ -32,14 +30,14 @@ It is now possible to access the daemonised container, for example using bash:
 CUDA
 ----
 
-Many images rely on [CUDA](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html). All images that rely on CUDA have a CUDA 7.5 version, with the "latest" tag on the Docker Hub. Some images also have CUDA 7.0 and 6.5 versions, with the "7.0" and "6.5" tags on the Docker Hub. The host must have the corresponding CUDA drivers (v352.39 for CUDA 7.5, v346.46 for CUDA 7.0, v340.29 for CUDA 6.5) installed for the kernel module. For a discussion of driver versions please see [issue #1](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/issues/1).
+Many images rely on [CUDA](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html). All images that rely on CUDA have a CUDA 7.5 version, unless specified otherwise, with the "latest" tag on the Docker Hub (an additional "7.5" tag will be available once [issue #341](https://github.com/docker/hub-feedback/issues/341) is sorted). Some images also have CUDA 7.0 and 6.5 versions, with the "7.0" and "6.5" tags on the Docker Hub. The host must have the corresponding CUDA drivers (v352.39 for CUDA 7.5, v346.46 for CUDA 7.0, v340.29 for CUDA 6.5) installed for the kernel module. For a discussion of driver versions please see [issue #1](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/issues/1).
 
-The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly. Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`. With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.

 Helpers

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit 004fbd19a69fec1704e7fa48773df0069c4abfb1
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Fri Sep 11 16:34:32 2015 +0100

    Update neon to v1.0
---
 cuda-neon/Dockerfile | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 cuda-neon/README.md  | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 neon/Dockerfile      |  6 ++++--
 3 files changed, 42 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cuda-neon/Dockerfile b/cuda-neon/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..de5e043
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+# Start with Ubuntu base image
+FROM cuda:latest
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install build-essential, git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  build-essential \
+  git \
+  python-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  python-virtualenv \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libyaml-dev
+
+# Clone neon repo and move into it
+RUN cd /root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" sysinstall
+# Set ~/neon as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/neon
diff --git a/cuda-neon/README.md b/cuda-neon/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..191e4eb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+cuda-neon
+=========
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 7.5.18](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [neon](http://neon.nervanasys.com/).
+
+Requirements
+------------
+
+- Host with corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 352.39) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-neon`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).
diff --git a/neon/Dockerfile b/neon/Dockerfile
index 3e9d34d..8cd28eb 100644
--- a/neon/Dockerfile
+++ b/neon/Dockerfile
@@ -8,11 +8,13 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   git \
   python-dev \
   python-pip \
-  python-numpy
+  python-virtualenv \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libyaml-dev
 
 # Clone neon repo and move into it
 RUN cd /root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
 # Make
-  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+  make -j"$(nproc)" sysinstall
 # Set ~/neon as working directory
 WORKDIR /root/neon

@@ -4,7 +4,17 @@ Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [Ceph](http://ceph.com).
 
 Usage
 -----
-**TODO**
+Setup involves a few steps:
+
+1. Generate a unique fsid with `genuuid` and save it (e.g. run ``export CEPH_UUID=`genuuid` `` on the host).
+1. Create `ceph.conf` with the fsid and move it to /etc/ceph/ceph.conf on the host.
+1. Run `docker run --net=host -v /etc/ceph:/etc/ceph -v /var/lib/ceph:/var/lib/ceph -e MON_IP=<public host IP> -e UUID=<ceph fsid> -it kaixhin/ceph` to start the container.
+1. Set up authentication by running `/opt/auth.sh`.
+1. Generate the monitor map by running `/opt/monmap.sh`.
+1. Start a monitor by running `/opt/mon.sh`.
+
+

 Citation
 --------
diff --git a/ceph/auth.sh b/ceph/auth.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..f47fe65
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ceph/auth.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Create cluster keyring and generate monitor secret key
+ceph-authtool --create-keyring /tmp/ceph.mon.keyring --gen-key -n mon. --cap mon 'allow *'
+# Create administrator keyring, generate client.admin user and add user to keyring
+ceph-authtool --create-keyring /etc/ceph/ceph.client.admin.keyring --gen-key -n client.admin --set-uid=0 --cap mon 'allow *' --cap osd 'allow *' --cap mds 'allow *'
+# Add client.admin key to cluster keyring
+ceph-authtool /tmp/ceph.mon.keyring --import-keyring /etc/ceph/ceph.client.admin.keyring
diff --git a/ceph/monmap.sh b/ceph/monmap.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..9b98261
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ceph/monmap.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Generate monitor map
+monmaptool --create --add `hostname -s` $MON_IP --fsid $UUID /tmp/monmap
+# Create default data directory on monitor host
+mkdir /var/lib/ceph/min/ceph-`hostname -s`
diff --git a/ceph/popmon.sh b/ceph/popmon.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..15f64cc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ceph/popmon.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Populate monitor daemon with monitor map and keyring
+ceph-mon --mkfs -i `hostname -s` --monmap /tmp/monmap --keyring /tmp/ceph.mon.keyring
+# Mark monitor as created
+touch /var/lib/ceph/mon/ceph-`hostname -s`/done
+# Start monitor
+start ceph-mon id=`hostname -s`

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Aug 26 18:49:55 2015 +0100

    Attempt VNC helper
---
 helpers/README.md |  5 +++++
 helpers/vnc.sh    | 14 ++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 19 insertions(+)

diff --git a/helpers/README.md b/helpers/README.md
index 946327c..b17e9f5 100644
--- a/helpers/README.md
+++ b/helpers/README.md
@@ -7,3 +7,8 @@ ssh
 ---
 
 When daemonising the container, make sure to expose port 22, i.e. use `-p 22`. Once the container has started, run ``docker exec -it <container> sh -c "`cat ./ssh.sh`"`` in this folder to set a password for the root user and set up SSH.
+
+vnc
+---
+
+When daemonising the container, make sure to expose port 5901, i.e. use `-p 5901`. Once the container has started, run ``docker exec -it <container> sh -c "`cat ./vnc.sh`"`` in this folder to set up VNC with a password.
diff --git a/helpers/vnc.sh b/helpers/vnc.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..a7565bc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/helpers/vnc.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Install LXDE, VNC server and Firefox
+apt-get update && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y \
+  firefox \
+  lxde-core \
+  lxterminal \
+  tightvncserver
+# Set USER for VNC server
+export USER=root
+# Set password
+vncpasswd
+# Start VNC server
+vncserver :1 -geometry 1280x800 -depth 24

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>

    Add SSH helper

 3 files changed, 29 insertions(+)

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 259756c..f8b738a 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -36,6 +36,11 @@ Many images rely on [CUDA](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html). All
 
 The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly. Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`. With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
 
+Helpers
+-------
+
+The scripts in these folder can provide extra functionality, e.g. SSH, to daemonised containers. Further details are available in the folder's readme.
+
 Migration
 ---------
 
diff --git a/helpers/README.md b/helpers/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..946327c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/helpers/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+Helpers
+=======
+
+The scripts in these folder can provide extra functionality, e.g. SSH, to daemonised containers.
+
+ssh
+---
+
+When daemonising the container, make sure to expose port 22, i.e. use `-p 22`. Once the container has started, run ``docker exec -it <container> sh -c "`cat ./ssh.sh`"`` in this folder to set a password for the root user and set up SSH.
diff --git a/helpers/ssh.sh b/helpers/ssh.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..68f53f3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/helpers/ssh.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Ask for password
+echo "Please enter a password (and then press enter):"
+read password
+# Install OpenSSH
+apt-get update && apt-get install -y openssh-server
+# Set password
+mkdir /var/run/sshd && echo "root:$password" | chpasswd
+# Allow root login with password
+sed -i 's/PermitRootLogin without-password/PermitRootLogin yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
+# Prevent user being kicked off after login
+sed -i 's@session\s*required\s*pam_loginuid.so@session optional pam_loginuid.so@g' /etc/pam.d/sshd
+# Restart SSH daemon
+service ssh restart

commit d0875496f2aa6e32aff9f3424db40c87583c8741
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Aug 5 23:30:07 2015 +0100

    Update readme with CUDA info
---
 README.md | 12 ++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 12 insertions(+)

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 2667d78..27ac867 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -29,6 +29,18 @@ It is now possible to access the daemonised container, for example using bash:
 
 `docker exec -it <id> bash`
 
+CUDA
+----
+
+Many images rely on [CUDA](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html). 
+The master branch contains support for CUDA 7.0, with the "latest" tag on the Docker Hub.
+The cuda_v6.5 branch contains support for CUDA 6.5 on some images, with the "6.5" tag on the Docker Hub.
+The host must have the corresponding CUDA drivers (specified in readme files in the appropriate branch) installed for the kernel module.
+
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
 Migration
 ---------

------
-The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
-Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cudnn-digits`.
-With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
-
-For automatically mapping the DIGITS server port use `docker run -dP <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cudnn-digits` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
-For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:8080 <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cudnn-digits`.
-The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cudnn-digits bash`.
-
-Citation
---------
-If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).
diff --git a/digits/Dockerfile b/digits/Dockerfile
index ce90ec3..dacec6d 100644
--- a/digits/Dockerfile
+++ b/digits/Dockerfile
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   gfortran
 
 # Clone NVIDIA Caffe repo and move into it
-RUN cd /root && git clone --branch v0.11.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
+RUN cd /root && git clone --branch caffe-0.12 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
 # Install python dependencies
   cd python && \
   for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done && \

author: Kaixhin
message: Sort out VNC restarts for cuda-vnc
patch:
commit aec6220a97f96034c6150dd182fc9940ce14b97e
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Mon Jul 20 17:33:19 2015 +0100

    Sort out VNC restarts for cuda-vnc
---
 cuda-vnc/Dockerfile | 5 +++--
 cuda-vnc/vnc.sh     | 6 ++++++
 2 files changed, 9 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile b/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile
index 0a8642a..edfe5eb 100644
--- a/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile
+++ b/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile
@@ -17,5 +17,6 @@ RUN cat password.txt password.txt | vncpasswd && \
 # Expose VNC port
 EXPOSE 5901
 
-# Run VNC server with tail in the foreground (ignores failure on restart)
-CMD vncserver :1 -geometry 1280x800 -depth 24 && tail -F /root/.vnc/*.log
+# Copy VNC script that handles restarts
+COPY vnc.sh /opt/
+CMD ["/opt/vnc.sh"]
diff --git a/cuda-vnc/vnc.sh b/cuda-vnc/vnc.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..15ee63c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-vnc/vnc.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Remove VNC lock (if process already killed)
+rm /tmp/.X1-lock /tmp/.X11-unix/X1
+# Run VNC server with tail in the foreground
+vncserver :1 -geometry 1280x800 -depth 24 && tail -F /root/.vnc/*.log

author: Kaixhin
message: Update readme with info on daemonisation
patch:
commit 1e8f337040e1c4457325518c73f3166fe30403eb
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Fri Jul 3 16:30:03 2015 +0100

    Update readme with info on daemonisation
---
 README.md | 17 +++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 17 insertions(+)

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index adb0fa1..a02bd5d 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -7,6 +7,9 @@ All images are based on Ubuntu Core 14.04 LTS, built with minimising size/layers
 Dependencies are indicated left to right e.g. cuda-vnc is VNC built on top of CUDA. Explicit dependencies are excluded.
 CUDA images can be used without NVIDIA devices attached, but will obviously lack the ability to use CUDA capabilities.
 
+Up-to-date builds
+-----------------
+
 Some builds based on certain software have builds that are triggered on schedule via a cron script to stay up to date on a weekly basis. These are:
 
 - [Caffe](https://github.com/BVLC/caffe)
@@ -15,4 +18,18 @@ Some builds based on certain software have builds that are triggered on schedule
 - [Theano](https://github.com/Theano/Theano)
 - [Torch](https://github.com/torch/distro)
 
+Daemonising containers
+----------------------
+
+Most containers run as a foreground process. To daemonise such a container it is possible to use:
+
+`docker run -d <image> /bin/sh -c "while true; do :; done"`
+
+It is now possible to access the daemonised container, for example using bash:
+
+`docker exec -it <id> bash`
+
+Research
+--------
+
 If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 29 23:20:53 2015 +0100

    Add NEURON
---
 neuron/Dockerfile | 13 +++++++++++++
 neuron/README.md  | 19 +++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 32 insertions(+)

diff --git a/neuron/Dockerfile b/neuron/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..3ed1e37
--- /dev/null
+++ b/neuron/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Start with VNC base image
+FROM kaixhin/vnc
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install wget
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget
+
+# Download NEURON (64-bit) package
+RUN wget http://www.neuron.yale.edu/ftp/neuron/versions/v7.4/nrn-7.4.x86_64.deb && \
+# Install
+  dpkg -i nrn-7.4.x86_64.deb && \
+# Clean up
+  rm nrn-7.4.x86_64.deb
diff --git a/neuron/README.md b/neuron/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..3b5565e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/neuron/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+neuron
+======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser + TightVNC server + [NEURON](http://www.neuron.yale.edu/neuron/). Runs as a daemon by default by using tail.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include password.txt with the password for TightVNC (by default this is "password"). This must be at least 8 characters and is truncated if longer.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The default password should be changed. To do so start up a container and then run `docker exec <id> bash -c "echo -e '<password>\n<password>\nn' | vncpasswd"`.
+
+For automatically mapping a VNC port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/neuron` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:5901 kaixhin/neuron`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/neuron bash`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 24 20:13:16 2015 +0100

    Add Torch+ Dockerfile
---

 3 files changed, 63 insertions(+)

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a3ceb12
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-torch+/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+# Start with CUDA Torch base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda-torch
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Move cuDNN over and extract
+ADD cudnn*.tgz root/
+# Install cuDNN
+RUN cd root/cudnn* && \
+  cp *.h /usr/local/cuda/include/ && \
+  cp *.so* /usr/local/cuda/lib64/
+
+# Install Folly, fbthrift, thpp and fblualib
+RUN curl -sk https://raw.githubusercontent.com/soumith/fblualib/master/install_all.sh | bash -e
+
+# Clone nn repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/torch/nn && cd nn && \
+# Checkout getParamsByDevice branch
+  git checkout getParamsByDevice && \
+# Make
+  luarocks make rocks/nn-scm-1.rockspec
+
+# Clone fbtorch repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/facebook/fbtorch.git && cd fbtorch && \
+# Make
+  luarocks make rocks/fbtorch-scm-1.rockspec
+
+# Clone fbnn repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/facebook/fbnn.git && cd fbnn && \
+# Make
+  luarocks make rocks/fbnn-scm-1.rockspec
+
+# Clone fbcunn repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/facebook/fbcunn.git && cd fbcunn && \
+# Make
+  luarocks make rocks/fbcunn-scm-1.rockspec
diff --git a/cuda-torch+/README.md b/cuda-torch+/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c906d6b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-torch+/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+cuda-torch+
+===========
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 7.0.28](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [cuDNN v2 RC3](https://developer.nvidia.com/cuDNN) + [Torch7](http://torch.ch/) (including iTorch). Includes the following additional packages:
+
+- [fblualib](https://github.com/facebook/fblualib)
+- [fbtorch](https://github.com/facebook/fbtorch)
+- [fbnn](https://github.com/facebook/fbnn)
+- [fbcunn](https://github.com/facebook/fbcunn)
+
+Requirements
+------------
+
+- Host with corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 346.46) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include the cuDNN v2 (Release Candidate 3) archive.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-torch+`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).

 As the image is intended to be lightweight, the CUDA samples were not installed. If you wish to experiment with the samples you will need to install them yourself. The steps are as below:

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit 38f980a154a70b56a291e749a27de252e21074b7
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Thu May 14 11:11:51 2015 +0100

    Fix cudanet-neon image
---
 cudanet-neon/Dockerfile | 26 +++++++++++++++-----------
 1 file changed, 15 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile b/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile
index f8417d5..db67bb6 100644
--- a/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile
+++ b/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile
@@ -16,20 +16,24 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   python-pip \
   python-scipy
 
-# Clone cuda-convnet2 repo (Nervana fork) and move into it
-RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/cuda-convnet2.git && cd cuda-convnet2 && \
-# Make build directory and move into it
-  mkdir build && cd build && \
-# Cmake
-  cmake .. && \
-# Make install
-  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+# Change to the /tmp directory
+RUN cd /tmp && \
+# Download run file
+  wget http://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/${CUDA_MAJOR_U}/Prod/local_installers/cuda_${CUDA_VERSION}_linux.run && \
+# Make the run file executable and extract
+  chmod +x cuda_*_linux.run && ./cuda_*_linux.run -extract=`pwd` && \
+# Install CUDA samples (silent) for helper_cuda.h
+  ./cuda-samples-linux-*.run -noprompt && \
+# Clean up
+  rm -rf *
 
 # Clone neon repo and move into it
 RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
-# Enable nervanagpu
+# Enable cudanet
   sed -i 's/GPU = 0/GPU = cudanet/g' setup.cfg && \
-# Make
-  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+# Make on single core (fails multithreaded)
+  make install
+# Add libraries to path
+ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH
 # Set ~/neon as working directory
 WORKDIR /root/neon

patch:
commit bc29cf7a54b2a70e514d43eaabff2b7cdc30a802
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed May 13 17:17:23 2015 +0100

    Add cudanet-neon
---
 cudanet-neon/Dockerfile    | 34 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 cudanet-neon/README.md     | 14 ++++++++++++++
 nervanagpu-neon/Dockerfile |  4 ++--
 3 files changed, 50 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile b/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4e1377d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudanet-neon/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  git \
+  libatlas-base-dev \
+  libjpeg-dev \
+  libopencv-dev \
+  python-dev \
+  python-magic \
+  python-matplotlib \
+  python-numpy \
+  python-pip \
+  python-scipy
+
+# Clone cuda-convnet2 repo (Nervana fork) and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/cuda-convnet2.git && cd cuda-convnet2 && \
+# Make build directory and move into it
+  mkdir build && cd build && \
+# Cmake
+  cmake .. && \
+# Make install
+  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+
+# Clone neon repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
+# Enable nervanagpu
+  sed -i 's/GPU = 0/GPU = cudanet/g' setup.cfg && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+# Set ~/neon as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/neon
diff --git a/cudanet-neon/README.md b/cudanet-neon/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bf5b466
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudanet-neon/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+cudanet-neon
+============
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 7.0.28](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [neon](http://neon.nervanasys.com/) ([cudanet](https://github.com/NervanaSystems/cuda-convnet2/) backend).
+Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 346.46) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cudanet-neon`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).
diff --git a/nervanagpu-neon/Dockerfile b/nervanagpu-neon/Dockerfile
index 5abbf34..473639c 100644
--- a/nervanagpu-neon/Dockerfile
+++ b/nervanagpu-neon/Dockerfile
@@ -7,8 +7,8 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   git \
   libboost-all-dev \
   python-dev \
-  python-pip \
-  python-numpy
+  python-numpy \
+  python-pip
 
 # Install PyCUDA and (upgrade) six
 RUN pip install --upgrade pycuda six

+  libatlas-dev \
+  python-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  python-nose \
+  python-numpy \
+  python-scipy
+
+# Set CUDA_ROOT
+ENV CUDA_ROOT /usr/local/cuda/bin
+# Install Theano
+RUN pip install Theano
+# Set up .theanorc for CUDA
+RUN echo "[global]\ndevice=gpu\nfloatX=float32" > /root/.theanorc
diff --git a/cuda-theano/README.md b/cuda-theano/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0def12b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-theano/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+theano
+======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 7.0.28](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [Theano](http://www.deeplearning.net/software/theano/).
+Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 346.46) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-theano`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).

patch:
commit c276862554d0bd705d6481b63191c76ea1b037b2
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Fri May 8 12:49:51 2015 +0100

    Add cuda-neon
---
 cuda-neon/Dockerfile | 39 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 cuda-neon/README.md  | 14 ++++++++++++++
 neon/Dockerfile      |  5 +++--
 3 files changed, 56 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cuda-neon/Dockerfile b/cuda-neon/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..99d4080
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
+Run apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  git \
+  libboost-all-dev \
+  python-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  python-numpy
+
+# Install PyCUDA
+RUN pip install pycuda
+
+# Clone MaxAs repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/maxas.git && cd maxas && \
+# Create makefile
+  perl Makefile.PL && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" && \
+  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+
+# Clone NervanaGPU repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/nervanagpu.git && cd nervanagpu && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)"
+
+# Clone neon repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NervanaSystems/neon.git && cd neon && \
+# Enable nervanagpu
+  sed -i 's/GPU = 0/GPU = nervanagpu/g' setup.cfg && \
+# Make
+  make -j"$(nproc)" install
+# Set ~/neon as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/neon
+
+# Run neon
+CMD ["neon"]
diff --git a/cuda-neon/README.md b/cuda-neon/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..80860bc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-neon/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@

+=========
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + [CUDA 7.0.28](http://www.nvidia.com/object/cuda_home_new.html) + [neon](http://neon.nervanasys.com/).
+Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 346.46) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-neon`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Citation
+--------
+If you find this useful in research please consider [citing this work](https://github.com/Kaixhin/dockerfiles/blob/master/CITATION.md).

@@ -2,12 +2,13 @@

 MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
 
-# Install build-essential, git, pip and python-dev
+# Install build-essential, git, python-dev, pip and other dependencies
 Run apt-get update && apt-get install -y \

commit d93d3f0d09d5b93c5118592a934547961f4bbbb6
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Tue May 5 16:30:12 2015 +0100

    Fix Torch images
---
 cuda-torch/Dockerfile | 6 +++++-
 cuda-torch/README.md  | 5 ++++-
 torch/Dockerfile      | 6 +++++-
 torch/README.md       | 5 +++++
 4 files changed, 19 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cuda-torch/Dockerfile b/cuda-torch/Dockerfile
index ff10e7e..733a6f8 100644
--- a/cuda-torch/Dockerfile
+++ b/cuda-torch/Dockerfile
@@ -11,8 +11,12 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
 RUN curl -sk https://raw.githubusercontent.com/torch/ezinstall/master/install-deps | bash
 RUN git clone https://github.com/torch/distro.git ~/torch --recursive
 RUN cd ~/torch; ./install.sh
+# Export paths
+ENV PATH=/root/torch/install/bin:$PATH \
+  LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$HOME/torch/install/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH \
+  DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=$HOME/torch/install/lib:$DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH
 # Set ~/torch as working directory
 WORKDIR /root/torch
 
 # Run Torch7 interactively
-CMD ["th", "-i"]
+CMD ["/root/torch/install/bin/th"]
diff --git a/cuda-torch/README.md b/cuda-torch/README.md
index 4ea603e..b8a51ea 100644
--- a/cuda-torch/README.md
+++ b/cuda-torch/README.md
@@ -5,5 +5,8 @@ Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 7.0.28 + Torch7 (including iTorch). Requires the host h
 Usage
 -----
 The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
-Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-torch`.
 With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Torch is started interactively using `docker run -it <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cuda-torch`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cuda-torch bash`.

 RUN git clone https://github.com/torch/distro.git ~/torch --recursive
 RUN cd ~/torch; ./install.sh
+# Export paths

author: Kaixhin
message: Update readme with info about cron builds
patch:
commit 89e751022573dc72048117e6ca2105748daf5fa2
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 19 10:05:59 2015 +0100

    Update readme with info about cron builds
---
 README.md | 6 ++++++
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index cf32209..5d6ff89 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -6,3 +6,9 @@ Compilation of Dockerfiles with automated builds enabled on the [Docker Hub](htt
 All images are based on Ubuntu Core 14.04 LTS, built with minimising size/layers and [best practices](https://docs.docker.com/articles/dockerfile_best-practices/) in mind.
 Dependencies are indicated left to right e.g. cuda-vnc is VNC built on top of CUDA. Explicit dependencies are excluded.
 CUDA images can be used without NVIDIA devices attached, but will obviously lack the ability to use CUDA capabilities.
+
+Some builds based on certain software have builds that are triggered on schedule via a cron script to stay up to date. These are:
+
+- Caffe (weekly)
+- DIGITS (daily)
+- Torch (weekly)

patch:
commit f5c2fc8ce37b9e16933b89fe00a248580ac72140
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 14 11:52:13 2015 +0100

    Add SSH with X server
---
 sshx/Dockerfile |  9 +++++++++
 sshx/README.md  | 11 +++++++++++
 2 files changed, 20 insertions(+)

diff --git a/sshx/Dockerfile b/sshx/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0d60d7a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/sshx/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Start with SSH base image
+FROM kaixhin/ssh
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install X server
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y xdm
+
+# Run SSH server without detaching
+CMD ["/usr/sbin/sshd", "-D"]
diff --git a/sshx/README.md b/sshx/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2c2d537
--- /dev/null
+++ b/sshx/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+sshx
+====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + SSH server + X server.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The default password ("password") should be changed. To do so start up a container and then run `docker exec <id> bash -c "echo 'root:<password>' | chpasswd"`.
+
+For automatically mapping a SSH port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/sshx` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:22 kaixhin/sshx`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/sshx bash`.

diff --git a/cudnn-digits/Dockerfile b/cudnn-digits/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f6d652d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudnn-digits/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Move cuDNN over and extract
+ADD cudnn*.tgz root/
+# Install cuDNN
+RUN cd root/cudnn* && \
+  cp cudnn.h /usr/local/include/ && \
+  cp -d libcudnn* /usr/local/lib/
+
+# Install git, bc and dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  git \
+  bc \
+  libatlas-base-dev \
+  libatlas-dev \
+  libboost-all-dev \
+  libopencv-dev \
+  libprotobuf-dev \
+  libgoogle-glog-dev \
+  libgflags-dev \
+  protobuf-compiler \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libleveldb-dev \
+  liblmdb-dev \
+  libsnappy-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  gfortran
+
+# Clone NVIDIA Caffe repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.11.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  cd python && \
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done && \
+  cd .. && \
+# Copy Makefile
+  cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
+# Make
+  make all && \
+  make py
+# Set environment variable
+ENV CAFFE_HOME /root/caffe
+
+# Clone DIGITS and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA/DIGITS.git digits && cd digits && \
+# Install dependencies
+  apt-get install -y graphviz gunicorn && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done
+
+# Expose server port
+EXPOSE 8080
+# Set ~/digits as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/digits
+# Start server
+CMD ["/root/digits/digits-server"]
diff --git a/cudnn-digits/README.md b/cudnn-digits/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5134a6b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudnn-digits/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+digits
+======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 7.0.28 + Pycaffe + DIGITS. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers (v. 346.46) installed for the kernel module.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include the cuDNN v2 (Release Candidate 3) archive.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-digits`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+For automatically mapping the DIGITS server port use `docker run -dP <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cuda-digits` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:8080 <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cuda-digits`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/cuda-digits bash`.
diff --git a/digits/Dockerfile b/digits/Dockerfile
index b12e2bf..182eb91 100644
--- a/digits/Dockerfile
+++ b/digits/Dockerfile
@@ -29,8 +29,9 @@ RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.11.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git &&
   cd .. && \
 # Copy Makefile
   cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
-# Enable CPU-only
+# Disable cuDNN
   sed -i 's/USE_CUDNN/# USE_CUDNN/g' Makefile.config && \
+# Enable CPU-only
   sed -i 's/# CPU_ONLY/CPU_ONLY/g' Makefile.config && \
 # Make
   make all && \

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 9 10:36:44 2015 +0100

    Add CPU-only DIGITS
---

 cuda-digits/Dockerfile | 57 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 cuda-digits/README.md  | 17 +++++++++++++++
 digits/Dockerfile      | 19 +++++++----------

index aa4137b..0eba715 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -1,2 +1,2 @@
 # Ignore cuDNN
-digits/cudnn*
+cuda-digits/cudnn*
diff --git a/cuda-digits/Dockerfile b/cuda-digits/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f6d652d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-digits/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Move cuDNN over and extract
+ADD cudnn*.tgz root/
+# Install cuDNN
+RUN cd root/cudnn* && \
+  cp cudnn.h /usr/local/include/ && \
+  cp -d libcudnn* /usr/local/lib/
+
+# Install git, bc and dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  git \
+  bc \
+  libatlas-base-dev \
+  libatlas-dev \
+  libboost-all-dev \
+  libopencv-dev \
+  libprotobuf-dev \
+  libgoogle-glog-dev \
+  libgflags-dev \
+  protobuf-compiler \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libleveldb-dev \
+  liblmdb-dev \
+  libsnappy-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  gfortran
+
+# Clone NVIDIA Caffe repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.11.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  cd python && \
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done && \
+  cd .. && \
+# Copy Makefile
+  cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
+# Make
+  make all && \
+  make py
+# Set environment variable
+ENV CAFFE_HOME /root/caffe
+
+# Clone DIGITS and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA/DIGITS.git digits && cd digits && \
+# Install dependencies
+  apt-get install -y graphviz gunicorn && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done
+
+# Expose server port
+EXPOSE 8080
+# Set ~/digits as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/digits
+# Start server
+CMD ["/root/digits/digits-server"]
diff --git a/cuda-digits/README.md b/cuda-digits/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..430f7f0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-digits/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+digits
+======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 7.0.28 + Pycaffe + DIGITS. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include the cuDNN v2 (Release Candidate 3) archive.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/digits`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+For automatically mapping the DIGITS server port use `docker run -dP <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/digits` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:8080 <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/digits`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/digits bash`.

-# Install cuDNN

-

   git \
@@ -29,13 +22,17 @@ RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   gfortran
 
 # Clone NVIDIA Caffe repo and move into it
-RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.10.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
+RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.11.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
 # Install python dependencies
   cd python && \
   for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done && \
   cd .. && \
-# Make
+# Copy Makefile
   cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
+# Enable CPU-only
+  sed -i 's/USE_CUDNN/# USE_CUDNN/g' Makefile.config && \
+  sed -i 's/# CPU_ONLY/CPU_ONLY/g' Makefile.config && \
+# Make

 # Set environment variable

    Begin Samba container

new file mode 100644

--- /dev/null
+++ b/samba/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+# Start with Ubuntu base image
+FROM ubuntu:14.04
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install samba
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y samba
+# Copy config files
+COPY *.txt /
+# Set password
+RUN echo -e "`cat password.txt`\n`cat password.txt`\n" > smbpasswd -s && \
+  # Make directory to share
+  mkdir /data && \
+  # Adjust samba configuration
+  cat smb.txt >> /etc/samba/smb.conf && \
+  # Stop samba
+  service smbd stop && \
+  # Clean up
+  rm *.txt
+
+# Expose samba ports
+EXPOSE 137 138 139 445
+# Run samba in the foreground
+CMD ["/usr/sbin/smbd", "-FS"]

+samba
+=====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + Samba server.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include password.txt with the password for smbd (by default this is "password").
+
+Usage
+-----
+The default password should be changed. To do so start up a container and then run `docker exec <id> bash -c "echo '<password>\n<password>\n' | smbpasswd -s"`.
+
+For automatically mapping a SSH port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/samba` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:137 -p <port>:138 -p <port>:139 -p <port>:445 kaixhin/samba`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/samba bash`.

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit a0959e721014fea9306bfc8e2e959b6c1914969c
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 18 18:20:59 2015 +0000

    Add DiGiTS
---
 .gitignore        |  4 ++--
 digits/Dockerfile | 54 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 digits/README.md  | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 74 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index 4c434bf..aa4137b 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -1,2 +1,2 @@
-# Ignore DiGiTS
-digits
+# Ignore cuDNN
+digits/cudnn*
diff --git a/digits/Dockerfile b/digits/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1329a34
--- /dev/null
+++ b/digits/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Move cuDNN over and extract
+ADD cudnn*.tgz root/
+# Install cuDNN
+RUN cd root/cudnn* && \
+  cp cudnn.h /usr/local/include/ && \
+  cp -d libcudnn* /usr/local/lib/
+
+# Install git, bc and dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  git \
+  bc \
+  libatlas-base-dev \
+  libatlas-dev \
+  libboost-all-dev \
+  libopencv-dev \
+  libprotobuf-dev \
+  libgoogle-glog-dev \
+  libgflags-dev \
+  protobuf-compiler \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libleveldb-dev \
+  liblmdb-dev \
+  libsnappy-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  gfortran
+
+# Clone NVIDIA Caffe repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone --branch v0.10.0 https://github.com/NVIDIA/caffe.git && cd caffe && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  cd python && \
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done && \
+  cd .. && \
+# Make
+  cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
+  make all && \
+  make py && \
+# Set environment variables
+  export CAFFE_HOME=/root/caffe
+
+# Clone DiGiTS and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA/DIGITS.git digits && cd digits && \
+# Install dependencies
+  apt-get install -y graphviz gunicorn && \
+# Install python dependencies
+  for req in $(cat requirements.txt); do pip install $req; done
+
+# Expose development server port
+EXPOSE 5000
+# Set ~/digits as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/digits
diff --git a/digits/README.md b/digits/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..9d285cc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/digits/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+digits
+======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.19 + PyCaffe + DiGiTS. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include the cuDNN v2 (Release Candidate 3) archive.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/digits`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+Note that due to the CUDA requirement the server can not be started automatically.
+
+For automatically mapping a DiGiTS devserver use `docker run -itP <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/digits` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -it -p <port>:5000 <nvidia_devices> kaixhin/digits`.

+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install dependencies
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  libatlas-base-dev \
+  libatlas-dev \
+  libboost-all-dev \
+  libopencv-dev \
+  libprotobuf-dev \
+  libgoogle-glog-dev \
+  libgflags-dev \
+  protobuf-compiler \
+  libhdf5-dev \
+  libleveldb-dev \
+  liblmdb-dev \
+  libsnappy-dev \
+  python-pip \
+  gfortran
+
+# Clone Caffe repo and move into it
+RUN cd root && git clone https://github.com/BVLC/caffe && cd caffe && \
+# Make and test
+  cp Makefile.config.example Makefile.config && \
+  make all && \
+  make test && \
+  make runtest
+# Set ~/caffe as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/caffe
diff --git a/caffe/README.md b/caffe/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e6a5676
--- /dev/null
+++ b/caffe/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+caffe
+=====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.19 + Caffe. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/caffe`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.

message: Update password instructions for CUDA VNC
patch:
commit f9267be166210e684ed3284071ecf23c2df40791
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sun Mar 15 21:07:30 2015 +0000

    Update password instructions for CUDA VNC
---
 cuda-vnc/README.md | 4 +++-
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/cuda-vnc/README.md b/cuda-vnc/README.md
index 3611695..c8b1a85 100644
--- a/cuda-vnc/README.md
+++ b/cuda-vnc/README.md
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.19 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser + TightVNC serv
 
 Build
 -----
-Include password.txt with the password for TightVNC (by default this is "password"). This must be at least 8 characters.
+Include password.txt with the password for TightVNC (by default this is "password"). This must be at least 8 characters and is truncated if longer.
 
 Usage
 -----
@@ -12,6 +12,8 @@ The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work prope
 Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-vnc`.
 With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
 
+The default password should be changed. To do so start up a container and then run `docker exec <id> bash -c "echo -e '<password>\n<password>\nn' | vncpasswd"`.
+
 For automatically mapping a VNC port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/cuda-vnc` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
 For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:5901 kaixhin/cuda-vnc`.
 The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/cuda-vnc bash`.

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit 860105f87f80dc3acebc9beb705ea888c4e05ee8
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 4 22:56:07 2015 +0000

    Add SSH and CUDA SSH
---
 cuda-ssh/Dockerfile   | 21 +++++++++++++++++++++
 cuda-ssh/README.md    | 17 +++++++++++++++++
 cuda-ssh/password.txt |  1 +
 ssh/Dockerfile        | 21 +++++++++++++++++++++
 ssh/README.md         | 13 +++++++++++++
 ssh/password.txt      |  1 +
 6 files changed, 74 insertions(+)

diff --git a/cuda-ssh/Dockerfile b/cuda-ssh/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..afc2a72
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-ssh/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install OpenSSH
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y openssh-server
+# Set password
+COPY password.txt .
+RUN mkdir /var/run/sshd && \
+  echo 'root:`cat password.txt`' | chpasswd && \
+  # Allow root login with password
+  sed -i 's/PermitRootLogin without-password/PermitRootLogin yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && \
+  # Prevent user being kicked off after login
+  sed -i 's@session\s*required\s*pam_loginuid.so@session optional pam_loginuid.so@g' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
+  # Clean up
+  rm password.txt
+# Expose SSH port
+EXPOSE 22
+
+# Run SSH server without detaching
+CMD ["sshd", "-D"]

new file mode 100644

+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+For automatically mapping a SSH port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/cuda-ssh` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:22 kaixhin/cuda-ssh`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/cuda-ssh bash`.

 vnc

+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.19 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser + TightVNC server. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module. Runs as a daemon by default by using tail. 
 
 Build
 -----
diff --git a/vnc-ros/Dockerfile b/vnc-ros/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c252b70
--- /dev/null
+++ b/vnc-ros/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Start with VNC base image
+FROM kaixhin/vnc
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install wget
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget
+# Set up sources and keys
+RUN echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu trusty main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list && \
+  wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ros/rosdistro/master/ros.key -O - | apt-key add -
+# Install ROS desktop full with rosinstall
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  ros-indigo-desktop-full \
+  python-rosinstall
+# Initialise rosdep
+RUN rosdep init && rosdep update
+# Set up environment
+RUN echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" >> ~/.bashrc

new file mode 100644

--- /dev/null
+++ b/vnc-ros/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+vnc-ros
+=======
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser + TightVNC server + ROS Indigo Desktop Full. Runs as a daemon by default by using tail.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include password.txt with the password for TightVNC (by default this is "password"). This must be at least 8 characters.
+
+Usage
+-----
+For automatically mapping a VNC port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/vnc-ros` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:5901 kaixhin/vnc-ros`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/vnc-ros bash`.

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit 2ab261cadad2a8ab3d5a9c80fd509364b9d1309f
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 4 10:43:53 2015 +0000

    Add CUDA VNC
---
 cuda-vnc/Dockerfile   | 21 +++++++++++++++++++++
 cuda-vnc/README.md    | 17 +++++++++++++++++
 cuda-vnc/password.txt |  1 +
 3 files changed, 39 insertions(+)

diff --git a/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile b/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2465efd
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-vnc/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install LXDE, VNC server and Firefox
+RUN apt-get update && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y \
+  firefox \
+  lxde-core \
+  lxterminal \
+  tightvncserver
+# Set user for VNC server (USER is only for build)
+ENV USER root
+# Set default password
+COPY password.txt .
+RUN cat password.txt password.txt | vncpasswd && \
+  rm password.txt
+# Expose VNC port
+EXPOSE 5901
+
+# Run VNC server with tail in the foreground
+CMD vncserver :1 -geometry 1280x800 -depth 24 && tail -F /root/.vnc/*.log
diff --git a/cuda-vnc/README.md b/cuda-vnc/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..55497df
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-vnc/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+vnc
+===
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.19 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser with a TightVNC server. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module. Runs as a daemon by default by using tail. 
+
+Build
+-----
+Include password.txt with the password for TightVNC (by default this is "password"). This must be at least 8 characters.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -dP --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda-vnc`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.
+
+For automatically mapping a VNC port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/cuda-vnc` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:5901 kaixhin/cuda-vnc`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/cuda-vnc bash`.
diff --git a/cuda-vnc/password.txt b/cuda-vnc/password.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f3097ab
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cuda-vnc/password.txt
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+password

author: Kaixhin
message: Separate Torch7 and Torch7 + CUDA images
patch:
commit 99c4a21de18a7d3806021597560d289368d758c8
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sun Mar 1 14:17:06 2015 +0000

    Separate Torch7 and Torch7 + CUDA images
---
 torch-cuda/Dockerfile | 15 +++++++++++++++
 torch-cuda/README.md  |  9 +++++++++
 torch/Dockerfile      |  4 ++--
 torch/README.md       |  8 +-------
 4 files changed, 27 insertions(+), 9 deletions(-)

diff --git a/torch-cuda/Dockerfile b/torch-cuda/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..acd8a51
--- /dev/null
+++ b/torch-cuda/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install curl and dependencies for iTorch
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+  curl \
+  ipython \
+  python-zmq
+# Run Torch7 installation scripts
+RUN curl -sk https://raw.githubusercontent.com/torch/ezinstall/master/install-deps | bash
+RUN git clone https://github.com/torch/distro.git ~/torch --recursive
+RUN cd ~/torch; ./install.sh
+# Set ~/torch as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/torch
diff --git a/torch-cuda/README.md b/torch-cuda/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1518c0f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/torch-cuda/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+torch
+=====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.14 + Torch7 (including iTorch). Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`.

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit e077b3c0467e8d78fe45c7d01144fdee7fd9be5f
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sun Mar 1 12:02:07 2015 +0000

    Make CUDA image more efficient/smaller
---
 cuda/Dockerfile | 27 ++++++++++++++-------------
 1 file changed, 14 insertions(+), 13 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cuda/Dockerfile b/cuda/Dockerfile
index 13426d9..aaf55a6 100644
--- a/cuda/Dockerfile
+++ b/cuda/Dockerfile
@@ -1,29 +1,30 @@
 # Start with Ubuntu base image
+# As the kernel is shared the host and container must correspond
 FROM ubuntu:14.04
 MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
 
-# Set /tmp as working directory
-WORKDIR /tmp
 # Install wget and build-essential
-RUN apt-get update && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y \
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   build-essential \
   wget
 # CUDA version
 ENV CUDA_MAJOR=6.5 \
   CUDA_VERSION=6.5.14 \
   CUDA_MAJOR_U=6_5
-# Location of CUDA run file
-ENV CUDA_RUN http://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/${CUDA_MAJOR_U}/rel/installers/cuda_${CUDA_VERSION}_linux_64.run
-# Download run file, make executable and extract
-RUN wget ${CUDA_RUN} && chmod +x cuda_*_linux_64.run && ./cuda_*_linux_64.run -extract=`pwd`
+
+# The next few steps are combined to prevent storing the installers
+# Change to the /tmp directory
+RUN cd /tmp && \
+# Download run file
+  wget http://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/${CUDA_MAJOR_U}/rel/installers/cuda_${CUDA_VERSION}_linux_64.run && \
+# Make the run file executable and extract
+  chmod +x cuda_*_linux_64.run && ./cuda_*_linux_64.run -extract=`pwd` && \
 # Install CUDA drivers (silent, no kernel) and toolkit (silent)
-# As the kernel is shared the host and container must correspond
-RUN ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86_64-*.run -s --no-kernel-module && ./cuda-linux64-rel-*.run -noprompt
+  ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86_64-*.run -s --no-kernel-module && ./cuda-linux64-rel-*.run -noprompt && \
+# Clean up
+  rm -rf *
+
 # Add to path
 ENV PATH /usr/local/cuda/bin:${PATH}
 # Configure dynamic link
 RUN echo "/usr/local/cuda/lib64" >> /etc/ld.so.conf && ldconfig
-# Clean up
-RUN rm -rf *
-# Reset working directory
-WORKDIR /

author: Kaixhin
patch:
commit 3e6b05bf383e6cf6dd86518fa2ff8d294be102dd
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sun Mar 1 00:01:49 2015 +0000

    Add Torch7
---
 torch/Dockerfile | 14 ++++++++++++++
 torch/README.md  |  9 +++++++++
 2 files changed, 23 insertions(+)

diff --git a/torch/Dockerfile b/torch/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2dc95d7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/torch/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install curl and IPython (for iTorch)
+RUN apt-get update && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y \
+  curl \
+  ipython
+# Run Torch7 installation scripts
+RUN curl -sk https://raw.githubusercontent.com/torch/ezinstall/master/install-deps | bash
+RUN git clone https://github.com/torch/distro.git ~/torch --recursive
+RUN cd ~/torch; ./install.sh
+# Set ~/torch as working directory
+WORKDIR /root/torch
diff --git a/torch/README.md b/torch/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..318a1dd
--- /dev/null
+++ b/torch/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+torch
+=====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.14 + Torch7. Requires the host has the corresponding CUDA drivers installed for the kernel module.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.

Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 28 19:13:28 2015 +0000

    Add cuDNN
---
 .gitignore       |  4 ++++
 cudnn/Dockerfile | 16 ++++++++++++++++
 cudnn/README.md  | 13 +++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 33 insertions(+)

diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
new file mode 100644

--- /dev/null
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+notes.txt
+

diff --git a/cudnn/Dockerfile b/cudnn/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e4c85d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudnn/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Start with CUDA base image
+FROM kaixhin/cuda
+MAINTAINER kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Set /tmp as working directory
+WORKDIR /tmp
+# Move cudnn archive over
+ADD * ./
+# Move files (allows failure for automatic builds)
+RUN mv cudnn*/*.h /usr/local/cuda-6.5/include/ || true
+RUN mv cudnn*/*.so* /usr/local/cuda-6.5/lib64/ || true
+RUN mv cudnn*/*.a /usr/local/cuda-6.5/lib64/ || true
+# Cleanup
+RUN rm -rf *
+# Reset working directory
+WORKDIR /

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..48a882e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cudnn/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+cudnn
+=====
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + CUDA 6.5.14 + cuDNN. Has the same requirements as the base [CUDA image](https://registry.hub.docker.com/u/kaixhin/cuda/).
+
+Build
+-----
+Include [cuDNN](https://developer.nvidia.com/cuDNN) archive. Requires joining the CUDA Registered Developer programme.
+
+Usage
+-----
+The container must have all NVIDIA devices attached to it for CUDA to work properly.
+Therefore the command will be as such: `docker run -it --device /dev/nvidiactl:/dev/nvidiactl --device /dev/nvidia-uvm:/dev/nvidia-uvm --device /dev/nvidia0:/dev/nvidia0 kaixhin/cuda`.
+With 4 GPUs this would also have to include `--device /dev/nvidia1:/dev/nvidia1 --device /dev/nvidia2:/dev/nvidia2 --device /dev/nvidia3:/dev/nvidia3`.

patch:
commit f4d646be742542cb8e62a72ac0d37972cd46f758
Author: Kaixhin <design@kaixhin.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 28 17:38:21 2015 +0000

    Add VNC build
---
 vnc/Dockerfile   | 21 +++++++++++++++++++++
 vnc/README.md    | 13 +++++++++++++
 vnc/password.txt |  1 +
 3 files changed, 35 insertions(+)

diff --git a/vnc/Dockerfile b/vnc/Dockerfile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a6180b5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/vnc/Dockerfile
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Start with Ubuntu base image
+FROM ubuntu:14.04
+MAINTAINER Kai Arulkumaran <design@kaixhin.com>
+
+# Install LXDE, VNC server and Firefox
+RUN apt-get update && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y \
+  firefox \
+  lxde-core \
+  lxterminal \
+  tightvncserver
+# Set user for VNC server (cannot use USER)
+ENV USER root
+# Set default password
+COPY password.txt .
+RUN cat password.txt password.txt | vncpasswd
+RUN rm password.txt
+# Expose VNC port
+EXPOSE 5901
+
+# Run VNC server with tail in the foreground
+CMD vncserver :1 -geometry 1280x800 -depth 24 && tail -F /root/.vnc/*.log
diff --git a/vnc/README.md b/vnc/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c6d2faf
--- /dev/null
+++ b/vnc/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+vnc
+===
+Ubuntu Core 14.04 + LXDE desktop + Firefox browser with a TightVNC server. Runs as a daemon by default by using tail.
+
+Build
+-----
+Include a file called "password" with the default password for TightVNC. This must be at least 8 characters.
+
+Usage
+-----
+For automatically mapping a VNC port use `docker run -dP kaixhin/vnc` and `docker port <id>` to retrieve the port.
+For specifying the port manually use `docker run -d -p <port>:5901 kaixhin/vnc`.
+The shell can be entered as usual using `docker run -it kaixhin/vnc bash`.
diff --git a/vnc/password.txt b/vnc/password.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f3097ab
--- /dev/null
+++ b/vnc/password.txt
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+password

author: Kaixhin
